entries.title: Broad cleanup skips the deterministic audit registry loop
entries.symptom: A broad cleanup edits ad hoc files, omits registry refresh or pass proof, and leaves future agents without a durable receipt.

guard.command: dart tool/audit_registry.dart refresh

entries.notes: For cleanup/refactor work, also stamp a pass with focused verification proof before final handoff.

entries.title: Design-system primitives drift into aliases or feature-only buckets
entries.symptom: A design pass preserves duplicate concepts under local aliases instead of converging on a canonical global contract and Widgetbook coverage path.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check --category design

entries.notes: Use canonical, repair, unify, register, discard as the review taxonomy.

entries.title: Generated design and tool manifests are stale after wide refactors
entries.symptom: CI or local checks fail after a logic-safe refactor because generated inventories, context packs, Widgetbook directories, or tool manifest entries were not refreshed.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check --manifest-only

entries.notes: When generated source changes, run the owning generator/check instead of editing generated JSON by hand.

entries.title: Widgetbook launch failures are misdiagnosed as setup failures
entries.symptom: A Widgetbook run fails because dirty-checkout compile blockers exist, but the failure is treated as a Widgetbook setup or port issue.

guard.command: Inspect the first compile error from the Widgetbook run before changing Widgetbook setup.

entries.notes: Run Widgetbook from the `widgetbook/` package and clear reported source blockers first.

entries.title: Unclaimed organizer event data crashes on non-null host fields
entries.symptom: Explore or event cards assume organizer host fields are non-null even though unclaimed organizer data allows null host ownership.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_view_model_test.dart --plain-name 'unclaimed organizer'

entries.notes: Model nullable fields where schema allows null and provide display fallbacks.

entries.title: Parallel Flutter tests race on native-asset build outputs
entries.symptom: Running multiple `flutter test` processes concurrently can crash Flutter while copying `build/native_assets/macos/native_assets.json` into `build/unit_test_assets/NativeAssetsManifest.json`, even when the same test passes sequentially.

guard.command: Run Flutter analyzer and Flutter tests sequentially; parallelize non-Flutter JSON/node/git scanners instead.

entries.notes: If this crash appears, rerun the failed Flutter test sequentially before treating it as product-code failure.

entries.title: Parallel subagents fragment canonical ownership
entries.symptom: Parallel agents make independent edits to canonical docs, generated registries, audit receipts, or the same source files, causing merge conflicts and multiple local truths.

guard.command: node tool/agent/check_agent_readiness.mjs

entries.notes: Use disposable worktrees, disjoint write scopes, parent-owned integration, and record delegation outcomes with tool/agent/record_delegation_outcome.mjs.

entries.title: Enforcement assets drift from the rules they claim to prove
entries.symptom: A scanner, lint wrapper, manifest check, or CI step stays green while no active rule points to it, the rule has no doc anchor, the tool is only syntax/count checked, or the known-bad probe no longer proves the diagnostic fires.

guard.command: node tool/check_enforcement_integrity.mjs

entries.notes: Update rule enforcement entries, manifest role/rules/vacuityProof metadata, doc anchors, and baseline receipts in the same pass as enforcement changes.

entries.title: Explore cover promotion drops the first search result or misstates feed counts
entries.symptom: The featured event is always removed from the body even after search replaces its cover, the result label counts only visible body rows, or the map pill counts events that cannot produce map pins.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_view_model_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreFeedSectionState counts the full feed and only promotes a visible cover'

entries.notes: Cover presentation is explicit shared state; result count uses the original full feed; mappableEventCount includes exact-coordinate internal events only.

entries.title: Explore sticky day headers escape the event stream or break sliver geometry
entries.symptom: The final day header remains pinned over recommendations or the club directory, or a pinned header nested under an incompatible sliver group throws layoutExtent exceeds paintExtent while scrolling.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'sticky day headers release before trailing Explore lanes'

entries.notes: Primary Explore bounds only its pinned event slivers; compatibility callers keep pinning disabled and render inline day headers.

entries.title: Explore calendar-day fixtures change behavior near midnight
entries.symptom: A sparse-market test creates events with separate DateTime.now plus-hour calls; on a UTC runner near midnight the events land on different days and unexpectedly enable the This week strip.

guard.command: TZ=UTC flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreEventsSection renders event-first content' --concurrency=1

entries.notes: Calendar grouping fixtures must derive every event and availability calculation from one captured reference time and pin intended same-day events to one explicit calendar day.

entries.title: Explore map blanks when native Google Maps key validation fails
entries.symptom: The Explore map route opens but renders a blank native Google map canvas when the iOS SDK rejects the configured API key at runtime, even though static local config validation passes.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreMapScreen default uses resilient Catch pin map'

entries.notes: Keep Explore's primary event-pin selection path on the resilient Catch placeholder map unless native Maps key acceptance is verified in a runtime device loop.

entries.title: Consumer and Host product identity drifts across build surfaces
entries.symptom: A role builds with the wrong entrypoint, bundle/application id, Firebase registration, icon, capability, deep-link association, force-update policy, or routine release owner because target identity was duplicated across scripts and native configuration.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check platform:app-targets platform:verify-ios-release-identity platform:verify-android-release-identity

entries.notes: The gate rejects aggregate Android builds, wrapper flavor/entrypoint overrides, Apple configuration/Firebase drift, date-bearing iOS archive plist regressions, non-monotonic Apple builds, manual or explicit flavor/workflow signing overrides, development-signed exported iOS apps, a second IPA export after verification, Android AAB signer/compiled-role/Firebase/Maps/debug/package/version drift, and split Consumer/Host release ownership. Automatic signing may leave the pre-export archive development-signed; only the exported IPA is distributable proof and must have production entitlements with explicit get-task-allow=false. Its SHA-256 is checked again before that same IPA is uploaded. Xcode Cloud retirement resolves each app-scoped API workflow by its exact name (`Default`); product-prefixed CI status contexts such as `Catch | Default` and `Runner | Default` are not API workflow names. GitHub Actions is canonical; APP-TARGET-IOS-GITHUB-CUTOVER-001 and APP-TARGET-ANDROID-PLAY-001 keep external store proof honest.

entries.title: Host v2 source provenance or full-shell comparisons silently drift
entries.symptom: Host references point outside the repository, a pinned gallery has broken local dependencies, navigation chrome is masked, a focused capture is missing, or a known Today/Events mismatch grows while a whole-screen aggregate still appears acceptable.

guard.command: node --test tool/design/check_reference_screens.test.mjs && node tool/design/check_reference_screens.mjs --check

entries.notes: App Build Matrix regenerates the four primary Host full-shell captures and runs a focused strict comparison. Stable parity debt may use a looser regression ceiling, but the canonical threshold and debt id remain visible until the feature is actually implemented.

entries.title: Marketing route metadata drifts from static output
entries.symptom: A public marketing route, page metadata key, generated listing path, sitemap entry, robots tag, or postbuild static output changes without a matching route contract update.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check marketing:website-routes

entries.notes: Run this guard before component-first website review so static HTML, hydrated metadata, generated organizer routes, and review states share one contract.

entries.title: Published organizer pages are metadata-only or claim readiness comes from a fixture
entries.symptom: An indexable organizer route has no semantic server-rendered profile content or JSON-LD, or a public claim CTA is enabled without a current receipt bound to the selected Firebase project and exact claim-target plan.

guard.command: node --test website/scripts/postbuild.test.mjs website/scripts/checkOrganizerBuildOutputs.test.mjs website/scripts/generateOrganizerListings.test.mjs tool/organizer_intake/sync_claim_targets_to_firestore.test.mjs && npm --workspace catch-marketing run build

entries.notes: Firestore mode must create the read-only environment receipt before website listing generation; fixture mode remains preview-only.

entries.title: Demo inventory or internal organizer scores leak into public discovery
entries.symptom: Deployable organizer data contains catchDemo records, Home renders past, external, or out-of-market events, or public organizer cards expose the internal profile-strength heuristic as a quality percentage.

guard.command: node --test website/scripts/generateOrganizerListings.test.mjs && npm --workspace catch-marketing run check:event-eligibility && npm --workspace catch-marketing run check:components && npm --workspace catch-marketing run build

entries.notes: Production generation excludes catchDemo by default; Storybook imports the explicit demo projection. Listing strength may rank internally but must not render as public reputation evidence.

entries.title: Production marketing deploy falls back to unavailable store links
entries.symptom: A production marketing Hosting deploy succeeds with empty, placeholder, non-HTTPS, or non-official App Store and Google Play product URLs.

guard.command: node --test tool/env/check_web_hosting_env.test.mjs && node tool/run.mjs check env:check-web-hosting

entries.notes: Local and preview builds may intentionally omit store links; production marketing predeploy requires official HTTPS product URLs and both deploy workflows forward the GitHub Environment variables.

entries.title: Production admin Hosting reaches an unusable Firebase Auth or App Check configuration
entries.symptom: The production admin sign-in screen loads, but phone or Google sign-in fails because the custom domain is unauthorized, the required Auth provider is disabled, or the deployed reCAPTCHA site key has no matching server-side Firebase App Check configuration.

guard.command: After production admin Auth, Hosting-domain, GitHub Environment App Check-key, or Firebase console changes, record live evidence that admin.catchdates.com and catchdates-admin.web.app are authorized Auth domains, Phone and Google providers are enabled, the deployed Firebase web app has a reCAPTCHA v3 server secret configured, and the live page completes App Check token exchange before sign-in.

entries.notes: The local Hosting env gate proves only that explicit project-aligned values exist. Console/API evidence plus a live browser/network smoke is required to prove remote Auth and App Check usability.

entries.title: Stale organizer claim decisions overwrite a newer ownership state
entries.symptom: A second active claim is accepted, or approving/rejecting an old request clears or replaces ownership established by a newer claim request.

guard.command: npm --prefix functions run build && node --test functions/lib/clubs/clubClaims.test.js functions/lib/admin/clubClaimReview.test.js

entries.notes: The club claim state owns the single active request id. Admin decisions must match it, and stale rejection may update only the old request plus its requester notification.

entries.title: Flutter, native, or notification product prose bypasses its reviewable catalog
entries.symptom: A new user-visible string is authored inline, catalog metadata is incomplete, generated native or notification copy drifts, or domain/controller state carries translated English instead of a semantic code and interpolation data.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check copy:mobile-catalog && node tool/run.mjs check copy:mobile-ownership && node tool/run.mjs check copy:native-sync && node tool/run.mjs check copy:notification-sync

entries.notes: The Flutter ownership baseline is explicit migration debt and may only shrink. The ARB and copy JSON catalogs are sources; generated Dart, InfoPlist.strings, and TypeScript are outputs.

entries.title: Native font packaging or semantic typography ownership drifts
entries.symptom: A Flutter target packages a web-only Archivo asset, app UI calls CatchFonts or owns letter spacing directly, or the generated typography contract loses family, case, alias, tracking, or variable-axis metadata.

guard.command: flutter test test/tooling/catch_ui_lints_typography_test.dart test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart tool/design/context_pack_builder_test.dart && flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos

entries.notes: Native Flutter bundles Archivo-Roman-VF.ttf and its OFL; web surfaces may retain WOFF2. Tracking is zero by default, with named uppercase roles and the welcome reel exception owned by CatchTextStyles.
